text: Sovereignty of the People and local elections in the Context of the Constitution of the Republic of Indonesia Year 1945 The phrase “democratically elected” contained in Article 18 paragraph (4) of the 1945 Constitution of the Republic of Indonesia is interpreted differently by different parties and the first opinion is about local elections that are directly elected and the second opinion about local elections that can be done by Parliament through the system of representation. If we see other articles relating to the election of people in the 1945 Constitution, the election of local leaders are not in the same category with the election of people in the parliament such as the DPR, DPD, Regency and City DPRD, the President and Vice President. Local elections through representation system done by Parliament is also can be considered democratic that also reflects the people souvereignty which is characterized by Pancasila, as aspired by the Founding Fathers.
explanation: The passage is coherent and academically styled with specialized legal terminology, which can be produced by both humans and AI. Some awkward phrasing and minor grammatical quirks hint at AI generation, though a non-native human author could produce similar results.
